name: Dashlane
tagline: Most feature-rich password manager with dark web monitoring
description: Dashlane packs the most features into a password manager, including premium dark web monitoring, a built-in VPN, and an automatic password changer.
longDescription: Dashlane differentiates itself with a feature set that goes well beyond password management. The Premium plan includes dark web monitoring (scanning for your personal information across breach databases), a built-in VPN for secure browsing on public Wi-Fi, and a unique password changer that can automatically update passwords on hundreds of supported websites. The interface is clean and modern, with excellent autofill accuracy. While Dashlane has removed its free plan for new users (replacing it with a limited free tier), the Premium and Family plans offer strong value given the breadth of included features.
cons: No permanent free plan for new users

keyFeatures.name: Dark Web Monitoring
keyFeatures.description: Continuously scans breach databases for your personal information. Sends real-time alerts if your email, passwords, or personal data appear in new breaches.

keyFeatures.name: Password Changer
keyFeatures.description: Automatically changes passwords on hundreds of supported sites without manual effort. Dramatically simplifies password updates after a breach.

keyFeatures.name: Built-in VPN
keyFeatures.description: Premium plan includes VPN protection for secure browsing on public Wi-Fi. Hotspot Shield-powered with servers in 20+ countries.

keyFeatures.name: Phishing Alerts
keyFeatures.description: AI-powered detection warns when you are about to enter credentials on a suspicious website. Prevents credential theft from convincing fake sites.

verdict: Dashlane is the best choice for users who want everything in one package. The dark web monitoring and automatic password changer are genuinely useful features, and the built-in VPN adds value that no other password manager matches. The premium price is justified by the breadth of features, but budget-conscious users should consider Bitwarden.

faqs.question: Does Dashlane still have a desktop app?
faqs.answer: No, Dashlane discontinued its desktop apps in 2022 and now operates as a web-first application with browser extensions and mobile apps. All functionality is available through the web app and extensions. If you prefer a native desktop experience, consider 1Password or Bitwarden.

faqs.question: How does Dashlane's password changer work?
faqs.answer: Dashlane's automatic password changer can update passwords on supported websites without manual intervention. It navigates to the password change page, generates a new strong password, and saves it — all with one click. It currently supports 300+ major websites.

author.name: Alex Chen
author.title: Senior Cybersecurity Analyst
author.bio: Alex has tested over 30 password managers across 5 years.

seoMeta.title: Dashlane Review 2026: 9.1/10 Rating | TrueTopReviews
